{

  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [LangChain Deep Agents: Build Agents for Complex, Multi-Step Tasks](https://www.langchain.com/deep-agents)\n2. [Deep Agents overview - Docs by LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview)\n3. [langchain-ai/deepagents](https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents)\n4. [Building Multi-Agent Applications with Deep Agents](https://blog.langchain.com/building-multi-agent-applications-with-deep-agents/)\n5. [Built-in components](https://docs.langchain.com/labs/deep-agents/built-in-components)\n\n# LangChain Deep Agents: Multi-Step Agent Architecture 2024\n\n## Overview\n\nDeep Agents is an open-source agent harness built on LangChain and LangGraph, designed for autonomous agents handling complex, multi-step tasks like research and coding.[1][2] It provides batteries-included capabilities rather than requiring manual configuration of prompts, tools, and context management.\n\n## Core Architecture Components\n\n**Planning and Task Decomposition**\nDeep Agents includes a built-in &#96;write_todos&#96; planning tool that enables agents to break down complex objectives, track progress, and adapt as they learn.[1][2]\n\n**Filesystem Backend**\nA virtual filesystem stores system prompts, skills, and long-term memory, with built-in tools for &#96;read_file&#96;, &#96;write_file&#96;, &#96;edit_file&#96;, &#96;ls&#96;, &#96;glob&#96;, and &#96;grep&#96; operations for context management.[1][3]\n\n**Subagent Delegation**\nThe architecture supports spawning specialized subagents for independent subtasks with isolated context windows, preventing context bloat. Subagents can run in parallel, use different models, or specialize in specific domains.[1][4] A built-in \"general-purpose\" subagent mirrors the main agent's capabilities for context isolation without specialized behavior.[4]\n\n**Context Management**\nNative middleware compresses conversation history, offloads large tool results, isolates context with subagents, and uses prompt caching to reduce latency and cost.[1]\n\n## Key Features\n\n- Model-agnostic with maximum configurability[1]\n- Durable execution via LangGraph runtime with streaming and checkpointing[3]\n- Shell access with sandboxing capabilities[3]\n- Available as both SDK and CLI[1]\n- Full LangSmith integration for tracing and deployment[1]\n\nDeep Agents is ideal when you need autonomous agents handling complex, non-deterministic, long-running tasks.[1]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: LangChain deep agent multi-step agent architecture 2024"
    }

```json
{
  "markdown": "\u003e Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://docs.langchain.com/llms.txt\n\u003e Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.\n\u003e \n\u003e ## Documentation Index\n\n# Deep Agents overview\n\n\u003e Build agents that can plan, use subagents, and leverage file systems for complex tasks\n\nDeep agents are the easiest way to start building agents and applications powered by LLMs—with built-in capabilities for task planning, file systems for context management, subagent-spawning, and long-term memory.\nYou can use deep agents for any task, including complex, multi-step tasks.\n\nWe think of &#96;deepagents&#96; as an [\"agent harness\"](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/concepts/products#agent-harnesses-like-the-deep-agents-sdk). It is the same core tool calling loop as other agent frameworks, but with built-in tools and capabilities.\n\n[&#96;deepagents&#96;](https://pypi.org/project/deepagents/) is a standalone library built on top of [LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/)'s core building blocks for agents. It uses the [LangGraph](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/) runtime for durable execution, streaming, human-in-the-loop, and other features.\n\nThe &#96;deepagents&#96; library contains:\n\n- [Deep Agents CLI](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/cli): A terminal coding agent built on the Deep Agents SDK\n- Deep Agents SDK: A package for building agents that can handle any task\n\n[LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/) is the framework that provides the core building blocks for your agents.\nTo learn more about the differences between LangChain, LangGraph, and Deep Agents, see [Frameworks, runtimes, and harnesses](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/concepts/products).\n\n## Create a deep agent\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# pip install -qU deepagents\nfrom deepagents import create_deep_agent\n\ndef get_weather(city: str) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Get weather for a given city.\"\"\"\n    return f\"It's always sunny in {city}!\"\n\nagent = create_deep_agent(\n    tools=[get_weather],\n    system_prompt=\"You are a helpful assistant\",\n)\n\n# Run the agent\nagent.invoke(\n    {\"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"what is the weather in sf\"}]}\n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee the [Quickstart](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/quickstart/) and [Customization guide](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/customization/) to get started building your own agents and applications with deep agents.\n\nUse [LangSmith](https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/home) to trace requests, debug agent behavior, and evaluate outputs. Set &#96;LANGSMITH_TRACING=true&#96; and your API key to get started.\n\n## When to use the Deep Agents\n\nUse the Deep Agents SDK when you want to build agents that can:\n\n- Persist memory across conversations and threads\n- Delegate work to specialized subagents for context isolation\n- Swap filesystem backends to use in-memory state, local disk, durable stores, [sandboxes](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/sandboxes), or [your own custom backend](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/backends)\n- Manage large amounts of context through file system tools\n- Handle complex, multi-step tasks that require planning and decomposition\n\nFor building simpler agents, consider using LangChain's [&#96;create_agent&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/agents) or building a custom [LangGraph](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/overview) workflow.\n\nUse the Deep Agents CLI when you want a coding agent on the command line, built on the Deep Agents SDK:\n\n- Execute code on your machine or in sandboxes.\n- Teach agents as you use them about your preferences, common patterns, and custom project knowledge.\n- Customize agents with skills and memory.\n- Run interactively or non-interactively — use the CLI as a chat-style coding agent, or pipe tasks with &#96;-n&#96; for scriptable, headless execution.\n\n## Core capabilities\n\n## Planning and task decomposition\n\nDeep agents include a built-in [&#96;write_todos&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/middleware/built-in#to-do-list) tool that enables agents to break down complex tasks into discrete steps, track progress, and adapt plans as new information emerges.\n\n## Context management\n\nFile system tools ([&#96;ls&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/harness#virtual-filesystem-access), [&#96;read_file&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/harness#virtual-filesystem-access), [&#96;write_file&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/harness#virtual-filesystem-access), [&#96;edit_file&#96;](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/harness#virtual-filesystem-access)) allow agents to offload large context to in-memory or filesystem storage, preventing context window overflow and enabling work with variable-length tool results.\n\n## Pluggable filesystem backends\n\nThe virtual filesystem is powered by [pluggable backends](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/backends) that you can swap to fit your use case. Choose from in-memory state, local disk, LangGraph store for cross-thread persistence, [sandboxes](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/sandboxes) for isolated code execution (Modal, Daytona, Deno), or combine multiple backends with composite routing. You can also implement your own custom backend.\n\n## Subagent spawning\n\nA built-in &#96;task&#96; tool enables agents to spawn specialized subagents for context isolation. This keeps the main agent's context clean while still going deep on specific subtasks.\n\n## Long-term memory\n\nExtend agents with persistent memory across threads using LangGraph's [Memory Store](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/persistence#memory-store). Agents can save and retrieve information from previous conversations.\n\n## Get started\n\n## SDK Quickstart\n\nBuild your first deep agent\n\n## Customization\n\nLearn about customization options for the SDK\n\n## Backends\n\nChoose and configure pluggable filesystem backends\n\n## Sandboxes\n\nExecute code in isolated environments\n\n## CLI\n\nUse the Deep Agents CLI\n\n## Reference\n\nSee the &#96;deepagents&#96; API reference\n\n---\n\n[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/langchain-ai/docs/edit/main/src/oss/deepagents/overview.mdx) or [file an issue](https://github.com/langchain-ai/docs/issues/new/choose).\n\n[Connect these docs](https://docs.langchain.com/use-these-docs) to Claude, VSCode, and more via MCP for real-time answers.",
  "url": "https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview"
}
```

```json
{
  "markdown": "Building Multi-Agent Applications with Deep Agents\n\nBy Sydney Runkle and Vivek Trivedy\n\nBreaking down complex tasks across specialized agents is one of the most effective approaches to building capable AI systems.\n\nDeep Agents makes this easy with two first-class primitives:\n\n- skills: progressively disclosing capabilities\n- subagents: delegating to isolated agents\n\nIn this post, we'll show you how to build multi-agent systems with Deep Agents.\n\n## Using Subagents: Specialized, Isolated Workers\n\nSubagents tackle a fundamental problem in agent engineering: context bloat. This is when an agent’s context window becomes close to full as it works on a task.\n\nWhy is this important? There’s great work from [Chroma on context rot](https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot?ref=blog.langchain.com) showing that models struggle to complete tasks as their context window gets filled. Our friends at HumanLayer call this high context regime the “ [dumb zone](https://www.hlyr.dev/blog/context-efficient-backpressure?ref=blog.langchain.com)”. Subagents isolate context from the main agent to help avoid quickly entering the dumb zone.\n\nWhen your agent makes dozens of web searches or file reads, the context window fills with intermediate results. Subagents isolate work by running with their own context window. So if the subagent is doing a lot of exploratory work before coming with its final answer, the main agent still only gets the final result, not the 20 tool calls that produced it.\n\nHere’s a look at the basic subagents architecture:\n\n### When to Use Subagents\n\n- Parallelization: Subagents can run simultaneously and return their outputs to the main agent. This reduces latency.\n- Multi-Model: Subagents can use different models than the main agent. For example, choosing a smaller model for lower latency.\n- Specialization: Use domain specific instructions or tools. Subagents developed by distinct teams can specialize in different verticals.\n- Context Preservation: A task requiring multiple steps can clutter the main agent's context (ex: codebase exploration).\n\n### Creating Subagents\n\nDefine subagents as dictionaries and pass them to&#96;create_deep_agent()&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfrom deepagents import create_deep_agent\n\nresearch_subagent = {\n    \"name\": \"research-agent\",\n    \"description\": \"Used to research more in depth questions\",\n    \"system_prompt\": \"You are a great researcher\",\n    \"tools\": [internet_search],\n    \"model\": \"openai:gpt-4o\",  # Optional: override main agent model\n}\n\nagent = create_deep_agent(\n    model=\"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929\",\n    subagents=[research_subagent]\n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee the [subagents](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/subagents?ref=blog.langchain.com#configuration) documentation for configuration details.\n\n### The General-Purpose Subagent\n\nDeep Agents include a built-in&#96;general-purpose&#96; subagent that mirrors your main agent's capabilities. It has the same system prompt, tools, and model. This is perfect for context isolation without specialized behavior.\n\nExample: Instead of your main agent making 10 web searches and filling its context, it can delegate to the general-purpose subagent with&#96;task(name=\"general-purpose\", task=\"Research quantum computing trends\")&#96;. The subagent performs all searches internally and returns only a summary.\n\n### Best Practices for Subagents\n\nWrite clear descriptions. Your main agent uses descriptions to decide which subagent to call:\n\n✅ Good:&#96;\"Analyzes financial data and generates investment insights with confidence scores\"&#96;❌ Bad:&#96;\"Does finance stuff\"&#96;\n\nKeep system prompts detailed. Include tool usage guidance and output format requirements:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nresearch_subagent = {\n    \"name\": \"research-agent\",\n    \"description\": \"Conducts in-depth research using web search and synthesizes findings\",\n    \"system_prompt\": \"\"\"You are a thorough researcher. Your job is to:\n\n    1. Break down the research question into searchable queries\n    2. Use internet_search to find relevant information\n    3. Synthesize findings into a comprehensive but concise summary\n    4. Cite sources when making claims\n\n    Output format:\n    - Summary (2-3 paragraphs)\n    - Key findings (bullet points)\n    - Sources (with URLs)\n\n    Keep your response under 500 words to maintain clean context.\"\"\",\n    \"tools\": [internet_search],\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMinimize tool sets. Only give subagents the tools they need:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n# ✅ Good: Focused tool set\nemail_agent = {\n    \"name\": \"email-sender\",\n    # Only email-related\n    \"tools\": [send_email, validate_email],\n}\n\n# ❌ Bad: Too many tools\nemail_agent = {\n    \"name\": \"email-sender\",\n    # Unfocused\n    \"tools\": [send_email, web_search, database_query, file_upload],\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Using Skills: Progressive Disclosure of Capabilities\n\nSkills provide a different pattern: progressive disclosure. Instead of giving your agent dozens of tools upfront, you define specialized capabilities in [SKILL.md](http://skill.md/?ref=blog.langchain.com) files. Your agent sees skill names and descriptions, then reads the full instructions only when needed.\n\nSkill descriptions are pre-loaded into the context window. The skill body is only loaded when the agent decides the skill is needed based on the description and previous context.\n\nCaption: skill descriptions are pre-loaded into the context window. The skill body is only loaded when the agent decides the skill is needed based on the description and previous context.\n\n### Setting Up Skills\n\nSkills use the [agentskills.io spec](https://agentskills.io/?ref=blog.langchain.com). Here's the structure:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n.deepagents/skills/\n├── deploy/SKILL.md\n└── review-pr/SKILL.md\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach [SKILL.md](http://skill.md/?ref=blog.langchain.com) file has YAML frontmatter with metadata and a main body:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n---\nname: deploy\ndescription: Deploy to production\nversion: 1.0.0  # Optional\ntags: [deployment, production]  # Optional\n---\n\n# Deploy to Production\n\nWhen the user asks to deploy, follow these steps:\n\n1. Run tests: &#96;npm test&#96;\n2. Build the application: &#96;npm run build&#96;\n3. Deploy to production: &#96;npm run deploy:prod&#96;\n4. Verify deployment: Check the health endpoint\n\nAlways confirm with the user before deploying to production.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Adding Skills to Your Agent\n\nUse the&#96;skills&#96; argument to&#96;create_deep_agent&#96; to load skills from the filesystem:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfrom deepagents import create_deep_agent\nfrom deepagents.backends import FilesystemBackend\n\nagent = create_deep_agent(\n    model=\"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929\",\n    backend=FilesystemBackend(root_dir=\"/\"),\n    skills=[\".deepagents/skills\"],\n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe agent now sees your skills. When it needs detailed instructions, it reads the full [SKILL.md](http://skill.md/?ref=blog.langchain.com) file.\n\nYou can also use other backends (such as a [StateBackend](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/backends?ref=blog.langchain.com#statebackend-ephemeral) or [StoreBackend](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/backends?ref=blog.langchain.com#storebackend-langgraph-store)), then invoke the agent with a&#96;files&#96; specification:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfrom deepagents.middleware.filesystem import FileData\n\n# default backend is a StateBackend\nagent = create_deep_agent(\n    model=\"anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\n    skills=[\"/skills/\"],\n)\n\nskill_content = \"\"\"\n---\nname: deploy\n...\n\"\"\"\n\n# Invoke the agent with the skill and virtual files\nresult = agent.invoke({\n    \"messages\": [HumanMessage(content=\"Research the latest Python releases\")],\n    \"files\": {\n        \"/skills/web-research/SKILL.md\": FileData(\n            content=skill_content.split(\"\\n\"),\n            created_at=\"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n            modified_at=\"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n        ),\n    },\n})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Choosing the Right Pattern\n\nHere’s a quick set of questions to guide you:\n\n| When you need to... | Use... | Both? |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Delegate complex, multi-step work | Subagents for context isolation |\n| Reuse procedures or instructions | Skills for progressive disclosure |\n| Provide specialized tools for specific tasks | Subagents with focused tool sets | ✅ |\n| Share capabilities across multiple agents | Skills (they’re just files) | ✅ |\n| Work with large tool sets | Skills to avoid token bloat |\n\n💡\n\nNote, this doesn’t have to be an either-or decision.Many systems use both. Skills define procedures; subagents execute complex multi-step work. Your subagents can use skills to effectively manage their context windows!\n\n## Next Steps\n\nTo learn more about multi-agent patterns in Deep Agents, check out our:\n\n- [Multi-agent patterns guide](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/multi-agent/index?ref=blog.langchain.com#choosing-a-pattern)- General guidance on choosing patterns\n- [Skills documentation](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/skills?ref=blog.langchain.com)- Detailed API reference and examples\n- [Subagents documentation](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/subagents?ref=blog.langchain.com)- Detailed API reference and examples\n\nThe key insight: multi-agent patterns don't have to be complicated. With the right abstractions (middleware for plumbing, tool calling for invocation), they become simple building blocks you can compose into capable, sophisticated systems.\n\nStart with subagents for context management, add skills for progressive disclosure, and build from there.\n\n### Join our newsletter\n\nUpdates from the LangChain team and community\n\nSubscribe\n\nProcessing your application...\n\nSuccess! Please check your inbox and click the link to confirm your subscription.\n\nSorry, something went wrong. Please try again.\n\n## Deploy agents instantly with Agent Builder templates\n\n[agent builder](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/agent-builder/) 3 min read\n\n## Now GA: LangSmith Agent Builder\n\n[agents](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/agents/) 2 min read\n\n## LangSmith Incident on May 1, 2025\n\n[Engineering](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/engineering/) 2 min read\n\n## Semantic Search for LangGraph Memory\n\n[langgraph](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/langgraph/) 3 min read\n\n## Reflection Agents\n\n[agents](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/agents/) 6 min read\n\n## Plan-and-Execute Agents\n\n[By LangChain](https://blog.langchain.com/tag/by-langchain/) 5 min read",
  "url": "https://blog.langchain.com/building-multi-agent-applications-with-deep-agents/"
}
```
User decision: **accepted**

Status: **completed**

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I now have a comprehensive view. Let me summarize:

**Your project (NorthClassVision agent):**
- **Orchestrator**: query → parse_goal (intent) → merge_followup → apply_clarification → compile_plan (goal → TaskGraph → schedule → PlanStep list) → dispatch (run tools) → generate_answer → build_response
- **Intent**: GoalSpec from LLM + rule fallback, clarification, followup merge
- **Plan**: SimpleRuleStrategy (subject×mode → tools) + ToTStrategy (LLM), TaskGraph with batches
- **Execution**: compile_plan produces PlanStep list, dispatch runs tools via runner
- **Memory**: InMemoryConversationStore, sliding window (6 turns), pending_goal for clarification
- **ReAct path**: ReActAgent with reason→tool_plan→execute→synthesize loop (separate from main compiler_v1 path)

**LangChain Deep Agents:**
- Planning: built-in `write_todos` tool for task decomposition, track progress, adapt as they learn
- Context: virtual filesystem (read_file, write_file, edit_file, ls, glob, grep) to offload large context
- Subagents: spawn specialized subagents with isolated context; general-purpose subagent for context isolation
- Memory: LangGraph Memory Store for cross-thread persistence
- Runtime: LangGraph for durable execution, streaming, checkpointing
- Skills: SKILL.md progressive disclosure (descriptions in context, full body on demand)

I'll write a concise comparison and actionable recommendations in 中文.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

根据查到的 LangChain Deep Agents 文档和你当前代码结构，整理成下面这份对比和可借鉴点。

---

## 一、LangChain Deep Agents 在做什么

Deep Agents 是建立在 LangChain + LangGraph 上的「agent  harness」，面向**复杂、多步、长会话**任务，核心包括：

| 能力 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **规划与任务分解** | 内置 `write_todos`：把复杂目标拆成步骤、跟踪进度、根据新信息调整计划 |
| **虚拟文件系统** | `read_file` / `write_file` / `edit_file` / `ls` / `glob` / `grep`，把大段上下文写出再按需读回，避免占满 context |
| **Subagent 委托** | 用 `task` 工具派发子任务到独立 agent，各自独立 context，可并行、可换模型、可专精领域；还有「通用子 agent」只做 context 隔离 |
| **长期记忆** | LangGraph Memory Store，跨会话/线程持久化 |
| **可插拔后端** | 文件系统可换成内存、本地磁盘、LangGraph Store、沙箱等 |

另外还有 **Skills**：用 SKILL.md 做「渐进式披露」——先给 agent 技能名+描述，需要时再读完整内容，减少 token 占用。

---

## 二、你当前架构（简要）

- **主链路**：`Orchestrator.query` → **intent**（parse_goal / merge_followup / clarification）→ **plan**（build_task_graph：SimpleRuleStrategy 或 ToTStrategy）→ **execution**（compile_plan → dispatch → run_tools）→ **normalizers**（generate_answer / build_response）。
- **记忆**：`InMemoryConversationStore`，滑动窗口 6 轮 + `pending_goal` 支持追问补槽。
- **备选**：`ReActAgent` 多轮 ReAct（reason → tool_plan → execute → synthesize），与 compiler_v1 并行存在。

你的路线是 **「意图 → 规划图 → 编译成步骤 → 一次性执行」**，Deep Agents 是 **「工具循环 + 内置规划/存储/子 agent 工具」**，思路不同但可以局部借鉴。

---

## 三、可以借鉴的几点（按优先级）

### 1. 规划可演进：类似 write_todos 的「步骤可见、可更新」

- **Deep Agents**：规划是 agent 通过 `write_todos` 工具维护的待办列表，执行中可增删改，而不是一次性定死。
- **你可做的**：  
  - 在 `TaskGraph` / `ExecutionPlan` 或现有 `PlanStep` 上增加「规划可见、可追加/勾选」的抽象（例如当前步骤列表 + 状态：pending/done/skipped）。  
  - 若以后接入 ReAct 或多轮循环，可加一个 **内部工具**：`update_plan(step_id, status, optional_result)`，让模型在观察到工具结果后能「打勾」或追加步骤，而不是每轮都重新从零规划。  
这样既不破坏你现在的「先规划再执行」的确定性，又为「执行中调整计划」留口子。

### 2. 大结果写出再按需读：虚拟文件系统思想

- **Deep Agents**：大块 tool 结果先 `write_file` 到虚拟盘，需要时再 `read_file`，主 context 只保留摘要或引用。  
- **你可做的**：  
  - 在 `extract_tool_results` / `generate_answer` 之前，对过长的 `tool_results` 做**摘要或截断**，只把摘要放进传给 LLM 的 context；  
  - 若单次请求会触发很多工具（例如多班级、多知识点），可引入「**结果缓存/存储**」：第一次调用把完整结果存起来（内存或 Redis），给 LLM 的只是 `summary` + `result_id`，若后续步骤需要再按 `result_id` 取回。  
这样既控制 token，又保留「按需取回」的能力，无需真的做完整文件系统。

### 3. 子 agent / 子任务隔离（对应 Subagent）

- **Deep Agents**：复杂、多步的子问题交给 subagent，主 agent 只拿到最终摘要，避免主 context 被 20 次工具调用塞满。  
- **你可做的**：  
  - 你已有 **TaskGraph + 多步执行**，可以定义「重活」为**子图**：例如「先 query_class trend，再 query_knowledge，再 query_student portrait」中，把「query_knowledge + query_student」打成一个子任务组，在 **execution** 层用单独的一次「子编排」执行（同一进程内即可），结果聚合成一个 **summary** 再交给 `generate_answer`。  
  - 接口上可以有一个 `run_subplan(steps, context)`，返回 `{ summary, detail_id }`，主流程只消费 `summary`。  
这样在不引入多进程/多模型的前提下，先实现「逻辑上的子任务 + 结果摘要」，以后若要真正隔离 context，再考虑单独进程或单独 LLM 调用。

### 4. 长期记忆与跨会话

- **Deep Agents**：用 LangGraph Memory Store 做跨 thread/会话的记忆。  
- **你可做的**：  
  - 你已有 `pending_goal` 和 `recent_turns`，可再增加「**用户级或班级级偏好**」（如常用班级、常用知识点），写入 `InMemoryConversationStore` 或单独的小表/Redis，在 `normalize_context` / `build_runtime_context` 里注入，供 intent 或 plan 使用。  
  - 若以后要跨会话，只需把 `InMemoryConversationStore` 换成「带持久化的 Store」接口，保持 `resolve_session_id`、`append_turn`、`set_pending_goal` 等 API 不变。

### 5. Skills 式「渐进式披露」（可选）

- **Deep Agents**：技能描述先进 context，完整 SKILL.md 只在需要时读入。  
- **你可做的**：  
  - 你已有 **subject × mode → 工具** 的映射和 ToT，可以把「能力描述」做成配置（例如每个 subject/mode 一段短描述），在 **intent/plan** 的 system 或 few-shot 里只带「简短能力列表」，只有在命中某类意图时再注入更细的说明或示例。  
  - 工具描述也可以「短描述 + 详细 schema 按需加载」，减少首轮 prompt 体积。

### 6. 可观测与可恢复（LangGraph 的 durable execution）

- **Deep Agents**：LangGraph 的 checkpoint、streaming。  
- **你可做的**：  
  - 你已有 `round_reasons`、`plan_steps`、`tool_results` 等，可以统一成一条 **trace**（例如每轮：intent → plan_steps → dispatch_batch → tool_results → answer），写入日志或可选的 trace 存储，便于调试和后续做「从某一步重试」。  
  - 若要做断点续跑，只需在 `dispatch` 或 runner 层对「已完成的 step」做持久化（例如 step_id + 结果），下次从「未完成的 steps」继续即可。

---

## 四、建议的落地顺序

1. **先做「大结果摘要 + 按需引用」**（对应上面 2）：改动小，立刻减轻 context 压力。  
2. **再做「规划可更新」**（对应 1）：在现有 PlanStep/TaskGraph 上加状态与可选更新接口，为多轮/ReAct 留扩展。  
3. **然后做「子任务摘要」**（对应 3）：在 execution 层划分子图或子编排，返回 summary 给主流程。  
4. **记忆与 Skills**（4、5）按产品需求再加；**持久化与 checkpoint**（6）在需要长任务或可恢复时再做。

你当前「意图 → 任务图 → 编译 → 执行」的设计已经和 Deep Agents 的「工具循环 + 内置规划」形成清晰分工，不必照搬；上述几点是在**不推翻现有架构**的前提下，从 Deep Agents 里挑最有用的思想做增强。如果你愿意，我可以按其中某一条（例如「大结果摘要」或「子任务摘要」）结合你仓库里的具体文件写出改法示例（含函数签名和调用点）。
